title: Mapping the US-Israeli war on Iran
author: Guardian News
fullText: This map shows all the strikes on Iran and across the Middle East over the past few days. What has been dizzying is how fast this war has become regional and how it might spread even more. The war started with the US and Israel launching a wave of air strikes across Iran. The bombings included a massive strike that killed the country's top supreme [music] leader Ali Kame as well as many of his officials. Iran's leaders believe they are in a battle for survival and have responded with fury. The Iranian [music] military has launched wave after wave of attacks, not only against Israel, but also across the whole Middle East, creating unprecedented chaos. [music] Bombings have been reported in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE, Jordan, [music] Iraq, and Kuwait. And in the confusion, three US F-15 fighter jets [music] were shot down over Q8 in what appears to be a friendly fire incident. [music] Iran says its goals are to target US military bases and US interests [music] in the region. But the strikes have also damaged major international hotels as well as civilian airports across the Gulf. It is unclear if these places have been specifically targeted [music] or if they were just hit by accident, but either way, the impact on air travel has been massive. Iran has also shut down a major maritime trade route [music] which has caused oil prices to sore. Separately, [music] Israel is conducting widespread bombing in Lebanon. This means another front has been opened in the war. Hezbollah, [music] which is an Iranian ally, had launched rocket attacks on Israel. Neighboring Europe is on edge. A British military base in Cyprus [music] has already been targeted by Iranian drones. Washington's allies in Europe did [music] not take part in the original attacks, and they worry that Trump has no clear objectives in this war. Still, [music] as the conflict spreads, they will feel pressure to get involved. That creates a dangerous [music] tinderbox situation in which more countries are dragged into the violence.
